# logs --follow streams completed runs with headers. We bound the
# follow with `timeout 2` whose 124 exit is the expected outcome, so
# we wrap the call in `!` for testscript.

exec eon add --cron '@every 1s' --name ticker -- echo tick

exec eon daemon &daemon&

# Let at least one run land before we start following.
exec sleep 2

# Follow for ~2s, then timeout fires (exit 124). The follower must
# have produced at least one header + body by then.
! exec timeout 2 eon logs ticker --follow
stdout '==> run #'
stdout 'tick'

exec eon stop
wait daemon
